title: senecio nemorensis subsp. stabianus
intro: Senecio nemorensis subsp. stabianus, also known as St. Baume's ragwort, is a subspecies of the wood ragwort plant (Senecio nemorensis) native to the Alps. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Senecio nemorensis subsp. stabianus include its ability to relieve pain and reduce fever, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
